name: Blackberry Jam Bars
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Plus 3 tbsp butter or margarine
recipeInstructions:
Cream the butter until smooth. Gradually add the sugar, beating well at medium speed of an electric mixer. Combine the flour, baking soda, and salt in a separate bowl. Add this dry mixture to the creamed butter and sugar, mixing thoroughly. Stir in the oats. Press half of the crumb mixture into a lightly greased 8-inch square pan. Spread the blackberry jam over the top, stopping about 1/4 inch from the edge. Press the remaining crumb mixture on top.
Bake at 400°F for 30 minutes or until firm and golden. Let cool completely before cutting into bars. Yield: About 2 dozen.
Microwave Directions:
Cream the butter until smooth. Gradually add the sugar, beating well at medium speed of an electric mixer. Combine the flour, baking soda, and salt in a separate bowl. Add this dry mixture to the creamed butter and sugar, mixing thoroughly. Stir in the oats. Press half of the crumb mixture into a lightly greased 8-inch square baking dish.
Shield the corners of the dish with triangles of foil, keeping the foil smooth and close to the dish. Microwave on MEDIUM (50% power) for 5 to 7 minutes or until firm, rotating the dish a quarter-turn at 2-minute intervals.
Spread the blackberry jam over the top, stopping about 1/4 inch from the edge. Press the remaining crumb mixture firmly on top. Continue microwaving with edges shielded on MEDIUM for another 11 to 13 minutes or until firm, rotating the dish a quarter-turn at 2-minute intervals.
Do not overcook; the mixture will continue to firm up as it cools. Allow to cool completely before cutting into bars.
